name: Blue Corn Tortillas
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the blue corn meal in a bowl and pour over the boiling water. Stir until well mixed.
2. Let sit for fifteen minutes, allowing the dough to rest.
3. Mix in half a cup of all-purpose flour into the dough on a floured surface.
4. Knead the dough for 2 to 3 minutes, incorporating more flour as needed until it is soft but not very strong.
5. Return the dough to the bowl and cover with a damp cloth. Let rest for 30 minutes.
6. Divide the dough into eight pieces. Between well-floured palms, shape each piece into flat round patties.
7. Heat your griddle over medium-high heat until it is hot.
8. On a floured surface, carefully roll out one tortilla to about 7 to 8 inches in diameter.
9. Cook the tortilla for approximately one minute on each side, resulting in brown flecks on both sides.
10. When cooked, remove the tortilla and wrap in a kitchen towel. Stack the tortillas one on top of another.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
